candidates.evidence_quote: Fluctuations in both average sales per location and average sales per productive square foot for fiscal 2024 generally tracked with comparable restaurant sales trends.
Average sales per location for The Cheesecake Factory restaurants open for the full year were approximately $12.4 million for fiscal 2024.

Average sales per productive square foot (defined as all interior square footage plus seasonally adjusted exterior patio square footage) for restaurants open for the full year were approximately $1,152 for fiscal 2024.
In fiscal years 2023 and 2024, we incurred higher construction costs, however we anticipate future costs to return to our targeted levels.
Restaurant-level preopening costs can fluctuate significantly from period to period, based on the number and timing of restaurant openings and the specific costs incurred for each restaurant.
grand opening publicity and other customer awareness activities that generate higher than usual customer traffic, particularly in new markets

our new restaurants have typically required a period of time after reaching normal traffic levels to achieve their targeted restaurant-level margins due to actual-to-theoretical food cost inefficiencies and labor productivity inefficiencies
due to actual-to-theoretical food cost inefficiencies and labor productivity inefficiencies commonly associated with new, highly complex restaurants such as ours
actual-to-theoretical food cost inefficiencies and labor productivity inefficiencies commonly associated with new, highly complex restaurants
Our licensees invest their capital to build and operate the restaurants, and we receive initial development fees, site and design fees and ongoing royalties based on our licensees’ restaurant sales.
we leverage opportunities in the consumer packaged goods channel by partnering with various third-party manufacturers
we believe there is potential for approximately 200 domestic locations over time, which supports our plan for approximately 20% average annual unit growth
The average check for each customer, including beverages and desserts, for fiscal 2024 averaged approximately $34.60 for lunch and approximately $44.40 for dinner.

Currently, we operate 38 Flower Child locations and believe there is potential for approximately 700 domestic locations over time, which supports our plan for approximately 20% average annual unit growth for this concept.
We target approximately 10% to 15% average annual unit growth for the aggregate Other FRC portfolio
We are evaluating beginning construction on a third bakery production facility in Charlestown, Indiana in fiscal 2025 or fiscal 2026.

We also leverage The Cheesecake Factory brand identity and utilize our bakery production capacity by selling cheesecakes and other baked products to external foodservice operators, retailers and distributors.
While we continue to operate in a competitive labor environment, we believe our people practices contribute significantly to our ability to attract talent
our ability to attract talent and to The Cheesecake Factory restaurants’ historically industry-leading retention rates

In recent years, we were impacted by geopolitical and macroeconomic events, causing supply chain challenges and significantly increased commodity prices. Our commodity environment began returning to more historical levels in fiscal 2024.
we were impacted by geopolitical and macroeconomic events, causing supply chain challenges and significantly increased commodity prices

our operating results were impacted by geopolitical and macroeconomic events, causing supply chain challenges and significantly increased commodity and wage inflation. Our commodity and wage inflationary environment began returning to more historical levels in fiscal 2024.

We attempt to negotiate short-term and long-term agreements for some of our principal commodity, supply and equipment requirements, such as certain dairy products and poultry, depending on market conditions and expected demand.
agreements for some of our principal commodity, supply and equipment requirements, such as certain dairy products and poultry
Adverse weather conditions and natural disasters may further exacerbate a number of these factors.
subject to volatility due to the relative availability of labor and distribution, weather, natural disasters, inventory levels and other supply and/or demand impacting events

We launched our Cheesecake Rewards ® program nationally in mid-2023 with the objective to leverage data analytics and insights to engage more effectively with our guests and drive incremental sales while maintaining our restaurant level margins.
contributing to our brand awareness and gift card sales, as well as our consumer packaged goods licensees on co-branded marketing campaigns
To raise awareness in the off-premise channel, we execute marketing campaigns with our third-party delivery provider and through our online ordering platform.

year-over-year comparisons can be significantly impacted by factors such as significant differences in year-over-year inflation, the number and timing of new restaurant openings and associated preopening costs, the timing of holidays, inclement weather
laws and regulations govern our operations as they relate to our staff members, including such matters as minimum wages, breaks, scheduling, exempt classifications, equal pay, overtime, tip credits
Dram shop litigation may result in significant judgments, including punitive damages.
Our food safety processes and systems are designed to mitigate the risk of contamination and illness and to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements as well as industry standards.
We utilize a web-based solution to efficiently contact our restaurants and monitor progress in the event of a product withdrawal or recall.

notes: Chunk contains a single event: 10-K 0001410578-25-000195 (2025-02-24), part 4 of 6 — Item 1 Business (unit economics, growth concepts, bakery, human capital, purchasing, marketing, regulation) plus the opening of Item 7 MD&A. No fiscal_kpis in this chunk.

Names follow DriverOntology.md: banned state/verb words (opening, growth) kept out of names — unit expansion claims coined as <brand>_unit_count with growth as state; 'beginning construction on a third bakery production facility' coined as bakery_production_capacity.

Brand/segment names (North Italia, Flower Child, Fox Restaurant Concepts segment 'Other FRC', Cheesecake Rewards program mark) used per brand-metric-is-own-driver rule; the parent legal name The Cheesecake Factory was never used in any driver_name.

Skipped as vague/boilerplate/redundant (12): restaurant site selection, landlord leasing terms, landlord construction contributions, media impressions, cybersecurity program (no causal claim in this part), 53rd-fiscal-week effect (period/number-bound name), bare geopolitical/macroeconomic events, bare year-over-year inflation, North Italia alcohol sales mix, commodity hedging (no contracts in place; folded into commodity_prices), third-party delivery provider (folded into off_premise_sales), immigration regulation (boilerplate regulatory list).
All 34 evidence quotes verified verbatim as exact substrings of the event content (script-asserted, including unicode apostrophes and registered-mark symbols).
